commands.repair_reason: - No automated repair lane is needed because the patch has no blocking findings and should proceed through normal maintainer and CI gating.; Cleared: The diff only adds doctor diagnostics and tests for existing plugin trust blockers; it does not broaden trust, execute new code, alter dependencies, or handle secrets differently. (sha=129099fd8fb3ba860874c9ce77beff51d1bae9f7)
commands.review_summary:
Adds doctor preview and channel-plugin blocker diagnostics for configured external Discord channel plugins blocked by missing explicit trust or restrictive `plugins.allow`, with focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +49, Tests +159. Total +208 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main skips the blocker scanner unless explicit plugin-disable config exists, so the configured external Discord state from the linked issue can miss the intended doctor warning; I did not run the CLI in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair lane is needed because the patch has no blocking findings and should proceed through normal maintainer and CI gating.

target.title: fix(doctor): warn for untrusted external Discord plugin
target.body:
## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Configured external channel plugins can be installed but blocked by missing explicit trust or a restrictive `plugins.allow`; doctor now reports that blocker instead of leaving setup guidance ambiguous.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on Node 24.15.0; live GitHub CI on pushed head `fdd4c356487128fad00069be55aa3d6481bc7431`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `CI=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 fnm exec --using v24.15.0 -- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor/shared/channel-plugin-blockers.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/preview-warnings.test.ts --reporter=dot --pool=forks --no-file-parallelism`
Evidence after fix: Focused tests passed: 2 files, 36 tests. Autoreview clean on the rebased head. GitHub PR checks show no non-green relevant checks.
Observed result after fix: Doctor preview warnings identify missing explicit plugin enablement and allowlist omission for configured external channel plugins.
What was not tested: Live Discord token startup; this change is limited to doctor diagnostics.

## Summary

- Adds doctor/setup diagnostics for configured external Discord channels when the installed external plugin is blocked by missing explicit trust or by a restrictive `plugins.allow` list.
- Preserves the explicit plugin trust boundary: external plugins are still not implicitly enabled; the fix only explains the blocker and the exact config repair.
- Wires the preview warning path for concrete configured channel surfaces even when there is no existing `plugins.entries.*` block.
- Adds focused regressions for missing `plugins.entries.discord.enabled=true` and `plugins.allow` omission.

Intentionally out of scope: live Discord token startup, implicit external plugin enablement, and unrelated channel/plugin setup changes.

Reviewers should focus on the channel-plugin blocker scan in `src/commands/doctor/shared/channel-plugin-blockers.ts` and the preview lazy-load gate in `src/commands/doctor/shared/preview-warnings.ts`.

## Linked context

Closes #83212

Related #83252, which is closed and unmerged. A fresh duplicate scan immediately before opening this PR found no open canonical fix for #83212.

Requested by the OpenClaw contribution worker task and aligned with the ClawSweeper guidance on #83212: diagnostic/setup repair around missing explicit enable/trust while preserving the trust boundary.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw doctor --non-interactive` now warns when `channels.discord` is configured and the external Discord plugin is installed but lacks explicit plugin trust/enabling. It also warns when Discord is omitted from a restrictive `plugins.allow` list.
- Real environment tested: local Windows checkout from this branch at `7afbc4f02a1789d7689ced36bfe299550cfa1dd6`, built with `pnpm build`, using isolated `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`. No live Discord token was used; only isolated config/CLI/doctor behavior was exercised.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Copy `extensions/discord` to an isolated external plugin directory.
  2. `node dist/entry.js plugins install --link <external-discord-copy> --dangerously-force-unsafe-install`
  3. Remove the installer-created `plugins.entries.discord` trust entry from the isolated config while preserving the plugin load path and installed state.
  4. `node dist/entry.js doctor --non-interactive`
- Evidence after fix:

```text
PLUGINS_INSTALL_EXIT=0
DOCTOR_EXIT=0
channelsDiscord=True
explicitTrustWarning=True
enablementHint=True
firstTimeSetupAbsent=True
```

- Observed result after fix: doctor output included `channels.discord`, `without explicit trust`, and `plugins.entries.discord.enabled=true`; it did not fall back to the misleading `Discord is in first-time setup mode` message for this installed-but-untrusted plugin state.
- What was not tested: no live Discord token, gateway startup, or Discord network roundtrip.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: Crabbox/Docker proof was attempted but did not produce valid behavior proof. Local-container smoke proof worked, but dirty worktree sync was blocked by missing host `rsync` on Windows. A clone-and-build proof from the pushed branch cloned the correct SHA but `pnpm build` was killed by SIGKILL during `tsdown` under local-container. A source/no-build CLI attempt reached `doctor` but ran the best-effort config/auto-enable path and hung in `openclaw-doctor`, so it is not counted as behavior proof.
- Before evidence: issue #83212 and the current upstream/main/release check showed the existing preview path could silently miss this installed-but-untrusted external Discord plugin state because blocker diagnostics were gated on explicit plugin blocker config.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

```bash
node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.commands.config.ts --configLoader runner src/commands/doctor/shared/channel-plugin-blockers.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/preview-warnings.test.ts src/commands/status-all/channels.test.ts --reporter=dot --pool=threads --maxWorkers=1
node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.plugins.config.ts --configLoader runner src/plugins/channel-plugin-ids.test.ts --reporter=dot --pool=threads --maxWorkers=1
git diff --check -- src/commands/doctor/shared/channel-plugin-blockers.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/channel-plugin-blockers.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/preview-warnings.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/preview-warnings.test.ts
pnpm --config.verify-deps-before-run=false check:changed --base upstream/main
pnpm build
codex review --uncommitted
```

Regression coverage added or updated:

- `channel-plugin-blockers.test.ts` now covers configured external Discord with an installed plugin missing explicit trust.
- `channel-plugin-blockers.test.ts` now covers configured external Discord omitted from restrictive `plugins.allow`.
- `preview-warnings.test.ts` now covers both warning paths through the doctor preview surface and verifies they do not show first-time setup mode.

What failed before this fix: the old scanner/preview gate skipped this diagnostic path when no explicit plugin blocker config existed, so configured Discord could remain blocked by the plugin trust policy without an actionable doctor warning.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`

Highest-risk area: plugin trust diagnostics around external channel plugins.

How that risk is mitigated: the change reports only installed, non-bundled channel plugins for channels that are concretely configured, and it reuses the existing configured-channel presence policy. It does not activate, trust, or allow external plugins automatically.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer review and CI.

Waiting on: GitHub Actions, maintainer review, and any maintainer-requested Docker/Crabbox rerun in an environment that can complete build/doctor proof.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: `codex review --uncommitted` found one real allowlist diagnostic gap; that finding was accepted, fixed, tested, and the review was rerun clean. No ClawSweeper re-review was requested.
target.author: brokemac79
